element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player must understand flight model fundamentals before meaningful interaction
Core simulation mechanic; realistic aerodynamic response requires learning curve
Geographic/waypoint systems unlock variety of scenarios
Advanced pilots layer environmental complexity for realism
Required for advanced scenarios; FAA-aligned certification context

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Self-paced with contextual intensity; tension follows real-world flight envelope (takeoff/climb moderate, cruise relaxed, approach/landing high). No scripted sequence; rhythm emerges from player decision-making and scenario selection.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Weather systems, advanced avionics, and complex airports available but optional; simpler aircraft and controlled conditions accessible to novices

difficulty_curve.early_game: Learning flight model behavior, basic controls, and simple VFR flight around default airport
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Cross-country navigation, basic IFR concepts, weather integration, multiple aircraft types
difficulty_curve.late_game: Complex IFR procedures, realistic weather scenarios, professional-grade avionics, FAA-certified training pathway
notable_spikes: First transition from VFR to IFR flight
Instrument approach procedures under simulated weather
Emergency procedure execution (engine failure, electrical loss)

notable_level_patterns.description: Implicit difficulty scaling through aircraft and scenario selection; users begin with simple aircraft (Cessna-class) in good conditions, advance to complex aircraft (Van's RV-10) and degraded weather/IFR conditions
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Default startup: Appleton International Airport with clear conditions and basic aircraft

notable_level_patterns.description: No forced sequence; player selects aircraft, airport, weather, and time-of-day independently. Tension arises from self-imposed objectives rather than scripted progression

Physics fidelity and aerodynamic accuracy allow infinite skill ceiling; casual players can succeed with simple approaches, serious enthusiasts and professionals can pursue FAA-certified training pathways
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Professional version enables structured IFR curricula aligned with real FAA standards
